dataset: Chinook Database (Sample digital media store database) with injected anomalies

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh copies chinook.db to this path. Live test confirmed: file exists, 890880 bytes, SQLite 3.x database with 13 tables.
Task description requires this exact connection name. Verifier checks data-sources.json for case-insensitive match. export_result.sh greps for 'LedgerAudit'.
Live test confirmed directory exists, owned by ga:ga, writable. setup_task.sh creates it via mkdir -p.
Live test confirmed directory exists, owned by ga:ga, writable. setup_task.sh creates it via mkdir -p.
Task description specifies export to /home/ga/Documents/exports/correction_report.csv. export_result.sh checks this exact path.
Task description specifies saving SQL to /home/ga/Documents/scripts/reconciliation.sql. export_result.sh checks this exact path.
Live database query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM invoice_items on original chinook.db = 2240. This is the standard Chinook database invoice_items count. After fix, count must return to 2240.
Live database query confirmed 18 duplicate invoice_items with InvoiceLineId 2241-2258. setup_task.sh inserts 6 per month (Oct/Nov/Dec 2013) via LIMIT 6 per month query.
Live query: SELECT InvoiceLineId FROM invoice_items WHERE InvoiceLineId > 2240. All 18 IDs confirmed. Original max InvoiceLineId = 2240.
Live query: GROUP BY month on duplicates. Each month has exactly 6 duplicates at $0.99 each = $5.94 excess per month. Verified via 06_dup_by_month.log and 06_duplicates.log.
Live query: SELECT DISTINCT InvoiceId FROM invoice_items WHERE InvoiceLineId > 2240. These 9 invoices have duplicated line items. Oct: 392,393,394; Nov: 399,400,401; Dec: 406,407,408.

From ground truth JSON verified during live test. These are the correct totals before the batch import error. After fix, each invoice Total must match: invoices with 2 original items = $1.98, invoices with 4 original items = $3.96.
Live query: SELECT * FROM invoices WHERE Total < 0 returns exactly one row: InvoiceId=413. Original Chinook max InvoiceId = 412, so 413 was inserted by setup.
Live query confirmed credit memo assigned to CustomerId=1 (Luis Goncalves, Brazil). Verified this customer has NO other Q4 2013 invoices (empty result set), confirming it was misapplied.
Live query: InvoiceId 413 has Total=-5.94. This exactly equals the negative of November's duplicate excess ($5.94), which is why November appears reconciled in GL comparison.
Live query: GL vs actual comparison. October GL=$37.62, actual=$43.56 (inflated by 6 duplicate items at $0.99 = $5.94).
Live query: November appears OK ($0.00 diff) because the credit memo (-$5.94) exactly offsets the duplicate excess (+$5.94). This is the key investigative twist.
Live query: December GL=$38.62, actual=$44.56. Same $5.94 excess from 6 duplicates.

pi_items.verified_value: import_log table has 3 rows: batch 1 (catalog refresh, COMPLETED), batch 2 (q4_invoice_correction, 18 records, FAILED), batch 3 (q4_invoice_correction_retry, 18 records, COMPLETED). Both batches 2 and 3 show 18 records targeting invoice_items, indicating the failed batch was retried and data was likely inserted twice.
pi_items.source: Live query: SELECT * FROM import_log. Verified all 3 rows with exact values from 06_import_log.log.
Live query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM general_ledger = 60. Covers 2009-01 through 2013-12 (5 years x 12 months).

Verified via dry-run simulation: after deleting 18 duplicates (InvoiceLineId > 2240), deleting credit memo (InvoiceId 413), and recalculating 9 invoice totals, ALL months reconcile with GL, invoice_items returns to 2240, no negative invoices remain.

pi_items.verified_value: Duplicates can be found via: SELECT InvoiceId, TrackId, COUNT(*) FROM invoice_items GROUP BY InvoiceId, TrackId HAVING COUNT(*) > 1. Returns exactly 18 pairs. Alternatively: SELECT * FROM invoice_items WHERE InvoiceLineId > 2240.
pi_items.source: Live query via 06_duplicate_detection_query.log confirmed both detection methods work correctly.

privileged_info_summary: The chinook_ledger.db database is a modified Chinook database with 13 tables (11 standard Chinook + general_ledger + import_log). The general_ledger table has 60 rows of correct monthly revenue. The import_log has 3 entries; batches 2 and 3 both show 18 records targeting invoice_items (the failed batch was retried, causing duplicates). There are exactly 18 duplicate invoice_items with InvoiceLineId 2241-2258: 6 in October 2013 (invoices 392,393,394), 6 in November (399,400,401), 6 in December (406,407,408). All duplicates are $0.99 each, creating $5.94 excess per month. Affected invoice totals were recalculated to include duplicates. A credit memo (InvoiceId 413, Total=-$5.94) was inserted for November, assigned to Customer 1 (Luis Goncalves, Brazil) who has no other Q4 2013 invoices. The credit memo perfectly masks November's discrepancy, so GL comparison shows only October (+$5.94) and December (+$5.94) as discrepant while November appears balanced. To fix: delete invoice_items where InvoiceLineId > 2240 (removes 18 duplicates), delete invoice 413 (credit memo), recalculate totals for invoices 392,393,394,399,400,401,406,407,408. Correct totals: invoices 392,393,399,400,406,407 should be $1.98 each; invoices 394,401,408 should be $3.96 each. After fix: invoice_items count = 2240, no negative invoices, all 60 months reconcile. The agent must also create a correction_log table, export correction_report.csv to /home/ga/Documents/exports/, and save reconciliation.sql to /home/ga/Documents/scripts/.
